About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker-long Dashboard Today's data shows LINC as the dominant performer, ranking #1 with an exceptional score of 94.4 driven by extraordinary long-term returns—2,444% over 10 years and 578% over 5 years, while maintaining all four moving average supports and outperforming SPY by 110% over the past year. LINC displays textbook momentum characteristics with a "clean" signal flag, strong ratio progression (particularly a 4.25x jump from 1y to 5y returns), and low volatility spike at 1.304x normal, signaling sustainable upward movement rather than speculation. In contrast, REX ranks second with a more moderate score, showing 1-year outperformance over SPY and QQQ but significantly weaker long-term gains (472% over 10 years versus LINC's 2,444%), and critically exhibiting a negative 99.35% return versus QQQ over the decade—a major red flag for relative weakness in tech-heavy markets. The correlation profiles differ notably: LINC clusters strongest with cyclicals (XLB materials at 0.336) suggesting secular industrial/commodity strength, while REX data appears truncated but shows similar sector exposure patterns. Overall, today's scan highlights LINC as a rare long-term secular winner
